description: Provides income support of ₹6,000 per year to all land-holding farmer families across India in three equal instalments of ₹2,000 every four months, directly transferred to their bank accounts.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year financial assistance in 3 instalments
Direct benefit transfer (DBT) to bank account
No intermediary, funds directly deposited
Covers all land-holding farmer families

description: Provides comprehensive insurance coverage for crop loss due to natural calamities, pests and diseases at extremely low premium rates. Farmers pay only 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi and 5% for commercial/horticultural crops.
benefits: Crop insurance at very low premium (1.5–5%)
Coverage for all natural calamities, pests, diseases
Post-harvest losses covered for 14 days
Technology-driven fast claim settlement
Cashless hospitalisation not applicable — financial compensation for crop loss

description: Provides short-term credit to farmers for agricultural inputs, post-harvest expenses, allied activities and personal consumption needs at concessional interest rates. Credit limit up to ₹3 lakh at 7% interest (effective 4% with subvention).
benefits: Revolving credit up to ₹3 lakh at 7% interest p.a.
Effective interest rate 4% with prompt repayment subvention
Covers seeds, fertilisers, pesticides, post-harvest expenses
Personal accident insurance cover of ₹50,000
Flexible repayment – aligned with crop seasons

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat – Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
description: World's largest government-funded health assurance scheme offering ₹5 lakh cashless hospitalisation cover per family per year for secondary and tertiary care at empanelled public and private hospitals.
benefits: Up to ₹5 lakh cashless hospitalisation per family per year
Covers pre and post-hospitalisation expenses
Over 1,393 medical procedures covered
Portable benefit across India – use in any empanelled hospital
No premium paid by beneficiary
application_centers: Any empanelled hospital Arogya Mitra desk

description: Provides financial assistance to BPL rural households to construct their own pucca houses. Beneficiaries receive ₹1.20 lakh (plain areas) or ₹1.30 lakh (hilly/difficult areas) with MGNREGS labour wages support.
benefits: ₹1.20 lakh grant in plain areas, ₹1.30 lakh in hilly areas
90/95 person-days MGNREGS wages for construction labour
Sanitation grant of ₹12,000 under SBM-G
Piped water connection under Jal Jeevan Mission
Direct bank transfer in instalments

description: National mission to ensure comprehensive financial inclusion by providing universal banking access. Every account comes with a RuPay debit card offering ₹2 lakh accidental insurance cover and ₹10,000 overdraft facility.
benefits: Zero-balance savings bank account
RuPay Debit Card with ₹2 lakh accidental insurance
Life cover of ₹30,000 for eligible beneficiaries
Overdraft facility up to ₹10,000
Direct benefit transfer for all government schemes
required_documents: Any government-issued photo ID (Voter ID / Passport / Driving Licence)

description: Provides collateral-free micro-enterprise loans under three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishore (₹50,001–5 lakh), and Tarun (₹5–10 lakh) to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises.
benefits: Collateral-free loans up to ₹10 lakh
Three tiers: Shishu (₹50K), Kishore (₹5L), Tarun (₹10L)
Competitive interest rates (linked to MCLR)
No processing fee for Shishu loans
Mudra RuPay card for business expenses
required_documents: Bank statements (last 6 months) for existing businesses
application_centers: Any public sector or private bank

description: Flagship skill training scheme enabling Indian youth to take industry-relevant skill training, get certified and earn better livelihood. Offers free short-duration skill training with monetary rewards and third-party certified assessment.
benefits: Free short-duration skill training (150–300 hours)
Monetary reward of ₹500–8,000 upon certification
Industry-recognized certification
Placement assistance after training
Training in 300+ job roles across 40+ sectors
application_centers: National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) training centres

description: A guaranteed pension scheme for workers in the unorganised sector. Subscribers receive guaranteed monthly pension of ₹1,000–5,000 after age 60 depending on contribution amount and age at joining.
benefits: Guaranteed monthly pension of ₹1,000–5,000 after age 60
Spouse receives same pension on subscriber's death
Nominee receives full corpus on spouse's death
Government co-contribution: 50% of contribution (max ₹1,000/year) for first 5 years
Available to all unorganised sector workers
required_documents: Mobile number linked with bank account
application_centers: Any bank branch with savings account

description: A one-year renewable life insurance scheme offering ₹2 lakh death cover at just ₹330 per year premium. Covers death due to any reason for people aged 18–50 years having a savings bank account.
benefits: ₹2 lakh life insurance cover at only ₹330/year premium
Covers death due to any reason (not just accident)
Auto-renewable annually
Simple enrolment through bank
Death claim directly to nominee's bank account

description: Accident insurance scheme offering ₹2 lakh for accidental death or total permanent disability and ₹1 lakh for partial permanent disability at just ₹20 per year for persons aged 18–70.
benefits: ₹2 lakh cover for accidental death / total permanent disability
₹1 lakh for partial permanent disability
Premium: only ₹20 per year
Auto-debit from savings account
Simple enrolment via bank or SMS

description: Facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore to at least one SC/ST borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up greenfield enterprises in manufacturing, services or trading sector.
benefits: Composite loan of ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore
Covers 75% of project cost
Repayment up to 7 years with moratorium of 18 months
Working capital component included
RuPay debit card for working capital
required_documents: Income proof / ITR (last 2 years if applicable)

description: Provides central assistance to urban poor to enable them to own pucca houses. Offers interest subsidy under Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS) – up to ₹2.67 lakh effective subsidy for EWS/LIG beneficiaries.
benefits: Interest subsidy up to ₹2.67 lakh (EWS/LIG)
Benami transactions prohibited – house in woman's name
Covers new construction, purchase and extension
Loan amount: EWS ₹6L, LIG ₹12L, MIG-I ₹9L, MIG-II ₹12L
Central assistance of ₹1.5 lakh for in-situ slum redevelopment

description: Provides social assistance to BPL elderly, widows, disabled persons and bereaved families. Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS) provides ₹200–500/month to persons aged 60+.
benefits: Old age pension ₹200/month (60–79 years)
Old age pension ₹500/month (80+ years)
Widow pension ₹300/month
Disability pension ₹300/month
One-time family benefit ₹20,000 on death of primary breadwinner
required_documents: Age proof (birth certificate / school certificate / Aadhaar)

description: National initiative to address declining child sex ratio and related gender discrimination by promoting the survival, protection and education of the girl child. Provides educational support and awareness programs.
benefits: Sukanya Samriddhi Account with 8.2% interest (tax-free)
Educational support for girl child
Scholarships and incentives for continuing education
Community awareness and gender sensitisation programs
Financial inclusion support for women
required_documents: Bank account / post office account details
application_centers: Post offices for Sukanya Samriddhi Account

description: Provides maternity benefit of ₹5,000 in three instalments to pregnant women and lactating mothers for the first living child, to partly compensate for wage loss and to improve health and nutrition outcomes.
benefits: Cash benefit of ₹5,000 in 3 instalments
1st instalment: ₹1,000 at early pregnancy registration
2nd instalment: ₹2,000 after minimum one antenatal check-up
3rd instalment: ₹2,000 after child birth registration and vaccination
Covers loss of wages during pregnancy and childbirth

description: Aims to reduce poverty by enabling poor households to access profitable occupations and social protection through Self-Help Groups (SHGs). Provides revolving funds, bank credit linkages and skill training to rural poor.
benefits: Revolving fund of ₹10,000–15,000 per SHG
Bank credit linkage: ₹1–10 lakh per SHG
Interest subvention: 7% or 4% for NE states
Skill development and employability training
Community managed social security through SHGs

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY)
description: Provides 5 kg of free foodgrain (rice/wheat) per person per month to all National Food Security Act (NFSA) beneficiaries, covering about 80 crore people across India under the One Nation One Ration Card scheme.
benefits: 5 kg free foodgrain (rice/wheat/millets) per person per month
Portable benefit under One Nation One Ration Card
No cost to beneficiary – completely free
Covers approx. 80 crore beneficiaries nationwide
Available at any PDS shop across India with Aadhaar
required_documents: Mobile number linked to Aadhaar (for OTP-based)

description: Provides free LPG connections to BPL women to protect health and reduce indoor air pollution from use of traditional cooking fuels. Includes free connection, first refill and hotplate to BPL households under SECC 2011.
benefits: Free LPG connection (deposit-free)
First refill cylinder free
Free hotplate/stove
Protection from indoor air pollution
Loan facility for first refill (repaid via refill subsidy)
required_documents: Aadhaar Card (applicant – woman of the household)
Ration card with family members listed
application_centers: Nearest LPG distributor (HP / Bharat / Indane Gas)

scheme_name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (MGNREGS)
description: Legally guarantees 100 days of wage employment per financial year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work. Provides unemployment allowance if work is not provided within 15 days.
benefits: Guaranteed 100 days of employment per household per year
Statutory minimum wages paid (state-specific)
Unemployment allowance if work denied within 15 days
Work provided within 5 km of household
Payment directly to bank/post office account within 15 days
required_documents: Job card (issued free by Gram Panchayat)
Bank account or post office account

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM)
description: Provides solar energy to the agriculture sector through installation of solar pumps and grid-connected solar power plants. Offers financial support to install solar pumps up to 7.5 HP individually or solar power plants on barren/fallow land, reducing electricity and diesel costs for irrigation.
benefits: 60% subsidy (30% central + 30% state) on solar pump installation
Solar pumps up to 7.5 HP capacity for irrigation
Farmers can earn additional income by selling surplus solar power to the grid
Reduces electricity and diesel costs for irrigation significantly
Covers installation of 25 lakh solar pumps under the scheme

description: Pan-India electronic trading portal that networks existing APMC mandis to create a unified national market for agricultural commodities. Allows farmers to sell produce online, enabling transparent price discovery through e-bidding and direct payments to bank accounts.
benefits: Online trading platform for 1,000+ agricultural commodities
Transparent price discovery through competitive e-bidding
Payments directly to farmer's bank account
Reduces post-harvest losses and dependence on middlemen
Online quality assay and integrated logistics support

description: Safe motherhood intervention scheme under the National Health Mission to reduce maternal and neonatal mortality by promoting institutional deliveries. Provides cash incentives to pregnant women for delivering at government or accredited private health facilities.

special: SC/ST women eligible in all states

benefits: Cash assistance ₹1,400 for rural institutional deliveries (in LPS states)
Cash assistance ₹700 for urban institutional deliveries
ASHA worker incentive ₹600 for each institutional delivery facilitated
Free antenatal check-ups and post-natal care
Free transport to hospital and back home

scheme_name: PM POSHAN Shakti Nirman (Mid Day Meal Scheme)
description: Provides free hot cooked meals to students in government and government-aided schools (Classes 1–8) to improve nutritional status, encourage school attendance and reduce classroom hunger. One of the world's largest school meal programmes covering over 11 crore children.

special: enrolled in government or government-aided school

benefits: Free hot cooked meal every school day
450 kcal and 12 g protein per meal for primary students
700 kcal and 20 g protein per meal for upper primary students
Covers approximately 11.8 crore children nationwide
Special enriched meals during harvest festivals and national holidays
required_documents: No separate application required – automatically provided to enrolled students
application_centers: Directly at enrolled government or government-aided school

scheme_name: PM SVANidhi – PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi
description: Provides affordable working capital loans to street vendors to resume livelihoods. Offers collateral-free loans of ₹10,000 (1st tranche), ₹20,000 (2nd tranche) and ₹50,000 (3rd tranche) with a 7% interest subsidy and digital transaction cashback incentives.

special: street vendor with vending certificate or letter of recommendation from ULB

benefits: Collateral-free working capital loan: ₹10,000 (1st), ₹20,000 (2nd), ₹50,000 (3rd tranche)
Interest subsidy of 7% per annum credited quarterly
Digital transaction incentive: cashback up to ₹1,200 per year
Credit history building through timely repayment
Linkage with social security schemes (PM-JAY, PMJJBY, PMSBY)
required_documents: Aadhaar Card and Voter ID or any government photo ID
Certificate of Vending or Letter of Recommendation (LoR) from ULB

description: Exclusive pension scheme for senior citizens aged 60 and above operated through LIC of India. Provides assured pension based on a guaranteed return of 7.4% per annum for 10 years with maximum investment of ₹15 lakh per senior citizen.
benefits: Assured pension at 7.4% p.a. for 10 years on purchase price
Flexible pension frequency: monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or annually
Purchase price fully refunded to nominee on subscriber's death during policy term
Loan up to 75% of purchase price available after 3 policy years
Free-look period of 15 days (30 days if purchased online) if not satisfied
required_documents: Age proof (birth certificate, school certificate, or Aadhaar)

description: National mission to provide safe and adequate piped drinking water through Functional Household Tap Connections (FHTC) to every rural household. Aims to supply 55 litres per capita per day of potable water at the household tap by 2024.
benefits: Free or heavily subsidised household tap water connection
55 litres per capita per day clean piped water supply
Pipeline infrastructure laid to village and household level
Water quality testing facilities established at Gram Panchayat
Solar-powered water supply systems in remote and hilly areas

description: National clean India mission to achieve Open Defecation Free (ODF) status in rural areas by constructing individual household toilets and community sanitation complexes. Provides ₹12,000 incentive to BPL and eligible households for toilet construction under ODF-Plus.

special: BPL / SC / ST / small and marginal farmers preferred

benefits: Incentive of ₹12,000 for constructing an individual household toilet
Technical support and model designs provided for toilet construction
Community Sanitation Complexes built at village level
Solid and liquid waste management infrastructure under ODF-Plus
Grey water management and plastic waste management facilities
required_documents: Bank account details for direct incentive transfer

scheme_name: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
description: Provides rooftop solar systems to one crore households offering up to 300 units of free electricity per month. Provides a subsidy up to ₹78,000 for a 3 kW system along with net metering, enabling households to earn bill credits by feeding surplus power to the grid.

special: own house with usable rooftop area

benefits: Up to 300 units of free electricity per month after installation
Central subsidy: ₹30,000 for 1 kW, ₹60,000 for 2 kW, ₹78,000 for 3 kW system
Additional income from surplus solar power via net metering
Collateral-free low-interest bank loan for remaining installation cost
System lifespan of 25 years with minimal maintenance cost
required_documents: Electricity bill with existing consumer number
Proof of house ownership or possession
application_centers: Approved solar vendors registered on the portal

description: Flagship scheme for fisheries sector development aiming to double fishers' income and generate employment. Provides subsidies for fish ponds, cage culture, sea cage farming, aquaculture units, fishing boats, and cold chain infrastructure to modernise the sector.
benefits: 40% subsidy for general category, 60% for SC/ST/women on eligible components
Subsidies for fish ponds, cage culture, and recirculatory aquaculture systems (RAS)
Support for fishing vessels, nets, and ice/fish storage boxes
Cold chain infrastructure and fish market development support
Fishers' group accident insurance of ₹5 lakh per person
required_documents: Caste certificate (if applicable for SC/ST/Women category)
Land ownership or lease documents for pond/aquaculture unit

description: Promotes apprenticeship training in industry by sharing stipend cost with employers. Government reimburses 25% of stipend (maximum ₹1,500/month per apprentice) directly to employers, encouraging industry to take on more apprentices and reduce youth unemployment.
benefits: Monthly stipend: ₹5,000–9,000 depending on educational qualification
Government reimburses 25% of stipend to employer (up to ₹1,500/month)
On-the-job industry training with nationally recognized certificate
Basic Training reimbursement of ₹7,500 per apprentice to employer
Access to 500+ designated trades and 40+ industry sectors

description: Provides quality generic medicines at affordable prices through a network of Pradhan Mantri Janaushadhi Kendras (Jan Aushadhi Stores). Medicines are offered at up to 90% discount compared to branded equivalents, making quality healthcare financially accessible.
benefits: Generic medicines at 50–90% cheaper than branded equivalents
Over 2,000 medicines and 300 surgical items available
9,000+ Jan Aushadhi Kendras across India
Same quality as branded drugs – manufactured under WHO-GMP standards
Surgical items, nutraceuticals and OTC products also stocked
required_documents: No registration required – open to all, walk-in access
application_centers: Nearest Jan Aushadhi Kendra (store locator at janaushadhi.gov.in)

description: Small savings scheme exclusively for the girl child offering one of the highest interest rates among government savings instruments (8.2% per annum, tax-free). Account is opened in the name of a girl child below 10 years and matures when she turns 21.
benefits: Interest rate 8.2% p.a. compounded annually (revised quarterly by government)
Fully tax-exempt: deposits, accrued interest and maturity amount under Section 80C
Minimum deposit ₹250/year; maximum ₹1.5 lakh/year
Partial withdrawal up to 50% for higher education expenses at age 18
Account matures when the girl turns 21 years of age
required_documents: Parent's or guardian's Aadhaar Card and PAN Card
application_centers: Authorised banks (SBI, BOB, BOI, HDFC, ICICI and others)

description: Pradhan Mantri Young Achievers Scholarship Award Scheme for Vibrant India – provides merit-based scholarships to OBC, EBC and DNT students enrolled in Classes 9–12 and undergraduate/postgraduate courses. Selected through a national YET entrance test.
benefits: ₹75,000 per year for students in Class 9 and 10
₹1,25,000 per year for students in Class 11–12 and higher education
Direct benefit transfer to student's bank account
Covers tuition fees, hostel, books and other academic expenses
Merit-based selection through national-level Young Achievers Entrance Test (YET)
required_documents: OBC / EBC / DNT caste certificate issued by competent authority
Income certificate showing family income below ₹2.5 lakh per year

scheme_name: Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme for SC Students
description: Provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste students studying at post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education without financial hardship. Covers maintenance allowance, tuition fees, study allowance and other academic expenses.
benefits: Maintenance allowance ₹380–1,200 per month based on course level and residence
Compulsory non-refundable fees reimbursed in full
Study tour charges and thesis typing/printing allowance for research scholars
Book allowance for students with visual impairment
Direct benefit transfer to student's bank account
required_documents: SC caste certificate issued by competent authority
Previous year mark sheet and pass certificate
Admission letter or fee receipt from institution
application_centers: District Social Welfare or SC & ST Welfare Office

description: Government initiative to promote innovation and startup growth in India by offering tax benefits, funding support, and simplified regulations for startups registered under DPIIT.
benefits: Income tax exemption for 3 years (Section 80-IAC)
Access to ₹10,000 crore Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS)
Self-certification for 9 labour and 3 environmental laws
Fast-track patent examination with 80% reduction in fees
Easy winding up – dissolution within 90 days under Insolvency & Bankruptcy Code
required_documents: Brief description of innovation and scalability of business

description: Provides farmers with soil health cards every two years containing crop-wise macro and micro nutrient recommendations, organic carbon status and soil pH to help improve soil fertility, reduce input costs and increase agricultural productivity.
benefits: Free soil testing for all farmers every 2 years
Crop-wise fertilizer and nutrient recommendations printed on the card
Reduces cost of cultivation by preventing over-use of fertilizers
Covers 12 parameters: NPK, macro and micro nutrients, pH, organic carbon
Online soil health card accessible at soilhealth.dac.gov.in
required_documents: Land ownership or cultivation details (Khasra number)

scheme_name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
description: Skill development program targeting rural youth aged 15–35 years to transform them into a skilled and globally competitive workforce. Offers free residential skill training with guaranteed placement, post-placement support and career progression assistance.
benefits: Free residential skill training (3–12 months) with boarding and lodging
Post-training guaranteed placement assistance
Post-placement support for 12 months
Industry-recognized certification
Mandatory minimum wage or above placement assured

scheme_name: National Food Security Act (NFSA) – Subsidised Grains
description: Legal entitlement ensuring subsidised food grains to approximately two-thirds of India's population (Priority Households and Antyodaya Anna Yojana families) through the Public Distribution System (PDS). PHH beneficiaries receive 5 kg/person/month at ₹1–3/kg; AAY families receive 35 kg/household/month.

special: Priority Household (PHH) or AAY ration card holder

benefits: Subsidised rice at ₹3/kg, wheat at ₹2/kg, coarse grains at ₹1/kg for PHH
35 kg food grain per month at same rates for AAY (poorest of the poor) families
Portable benefit under One Nation One Ration Card (ONORC)
Legal entitlement – grievance redressal mechanism mandated by law
Covers approximately 81.35 crore beneficiaries across India
required_documents: Aadhaar Card for biometric/OTP authentication at PDS shop
application_centers: State Food & Civil Supplies Department offices

description: National digital literacy mission targeting rural households to provide basic digital training so that at least one member per rural household can operate a computer or smart device, access the internet, and use digital payment tools and government services online.

special: one non-digitally literate member per household

benefits: Free 20-hour digital literacy training (spread over 10–20 days)
Nationally recognized certificate issued by NIELIT / CSC Academy
Covers computer basics, internet use, digital payments and e-government services
Training available in local Indian languages
No educational qualification required
application_centers: Common Service Centres (CSC) across rural India
